title: Arrays, Pointers, Arithmetic, and Bounds · Changed-Lifetime Lab
summary: An independent maintainer ports the bounded image-slice library to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded image-slice library with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.
Perform pointer arithmetic only within one array object or one-past it and avoid dereferencing one-past pointers.
State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Array Pointer Distinction
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 9501. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a half-open array slice before access; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Array Pointer Distinction.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Array Pointer Distinction, keep Array Pointer Distinction connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.
